confidence_rationale: Video is clearly identified as instructional/educational content from the SAGES Masters Series on Surgical Coaching. It contains presentation slides, lecture narration, frameworks (Wisconsin Framework), coaching philosophies, and educational diagrams. No actual FLS task performance is visible. No instruments, trainers, or procedural tasks are shown.
cannot_determine: Task performance metrics — no FLS task is being performed
Completion time — no procedure is executed
Penalties — no technical performance to evaluate
technique_summary: This is educational/lecture content, not a performance video. The content focuses on surgical coaching theory, coaching methodology, and peer feedback frameworks applicable to surgical training.

demonstrated_techniques: Definition of coaching vs. teaching paradigm
Wisconsin Framework for surgical coaching (coach knowledge, content, activities, interpersonal skills)
Peer coaching as distinct from expert instruction
Active listening and powerful inquiry as coaching activities
verbal_cues: Coaching unlocks potential rather than teaches
Peer coaching involves equal, non-competitive relationships
Coaching focuses on helping learners recognize what works and improve
Constructive feedback is responsive to coachee goals and issues
common_errors_addressed: Traditional teaching model assumes students no longer need instruction post-graduation
Rescue mentality (taking blame, making decisions for learner) vs. responsibility model (empowering learner)
Weakness-focused feedback vs. strength-based, learning-oriented feedback
Expert directive approach vs. co-learner curiosity approach
teaching_content.educational_focus: Surgical coaching framework and methodology, not technical FLS skill

note: This video presents the Masters Series on Surgical Coaching from SAGES and demonstrates coaching philosophy and framework. It is not a performance evaluation of any FLS task. No scoring is applicable.
